NKJV.1: Imitate me, just as I also imitate Christ.
NKJV.2: Now I praise you, brethren, that you remember me in all things and keep the traditions just as I delivered them to you.
NKJV.3: But I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, the head of woman is man, and the head of Christ is God.
NKJV.4: Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonors his head.
NKJV.5: But every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered dishonors her head, for that is one and the same as if her head were shaved.
NKJV.6: For if a woman is not covered, let her also be shorn. But if it is shameful for a woman to be shorn or shaved, let her be covered.
NKJV.7: For a man indeed ought not to cover his head, since he is the image and glory of God; but woman is the glory of man.
NKJV.8: For man is not from woman, but woman from man.
NKJV.9: Nor was man created for the woman, but woman for the man.
NKJV.10: For this reason the woman ought to have a symbol of authority on her head, because of the angels.
NKJV.11: Nevertheless, neither is man independent of woman, nor woman independent of man, in the Lord.
NKJV.12: For as woman came from man, even so man also comes through woman; but all things are from God.
NKJV.13: Judge among yourselves. Is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?
NKJV.14: Does not even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him?
NKJV.15: But if a woman has long hair, it is a glory to her; for her hair is given to her for a covering.
NKJV.16: But if anyone seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, nor do the churches of God.
NKJV.17: Now in giving these instructions I do not praise you, since you come together not for the better but for the worse.
NKJV.18: For first of all, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and in part I believe it.
NKJV.19: For there must also be factions among you, that those who are approved may be recognized among you.
NKJV.20: Therefore when you come together in one place, it is not to eat the Lord’s Supper.
NKJV.21: For in eating, each one takes his own supper ahead of others; and one is hungry and another is drunk.
NKJV.22: What! Do you not have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you in this? I do not praise you.
NKJV.23: For I received from the Lord that which I also delivered to you: that the Lord Jesus on the same night in which He was betrayed took bread;
NKJV.24: and when He had given thanks, He broke it and said, “Take, eat; this is My body which is broken for you; do this in remembrance of Me.”
NKJV.25: In the same manner He also took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in My blood. This do, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of Me.”
NKJV.26: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death till He comes.
NKJV.27: Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks this cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
NKJV.28: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
NKJV.29: For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy manner eats and drinks judgment to himself, not discerning the Lord’s body.
NKJV.30: For this reason many are weak and sick among you, and many sleep.
NKJV.31: For if we would judge ourselves, we would not be judged.
NKJV.32: But when we are judged, we are chastened by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.
NKJV.33: Therefore, my brethren, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
NKJV.34: But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest you come together for judgment. And the rest I will set in order when I come.

ESV.1: Be imitators of me, as I am of Christ.
ESV.2: Now I commend you because you remember me in everything and maintain the traditions even as I delivered them to you.
ESV.3: But I want you to understand that the head of every man is Christ, the head of a wife  is her husband,  and the head of Christ is God.
ESV.4: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered dishonors his head,
ESV.5: but every wife  who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered dishonors her head, since it is the same as if her head were shaven.
ESV.6: For if a wife will not cover her head, then she should cut her hair short. But since it is disgraceful for a wife to cut off her hair or shave her head, let her cover her head.
ESV.7: For a man ought not to cover his head, since he is the image and glory of God, but woman is the glory of man.
ESV.8: For man was not made from woman, but woman from man.
ESV.9: Neither was man created for woman, but woman for man.
ESV.10: That is why a wife ought to have a symbol of authority on her head, because of the angels.
ESV.11: Nevertheless, in the Lord woman is not independent of man nor man of woman;
ESV.12: for as woman was made from man, so man is now born of woman. And all things are from God.
ESV.13: Judge for yourselves: is it proper for a wife to pray to God with her head uncovered?
ESV.14: Does not nature itself teach you that if a man wears long hair it is a disgrace for him,
ESV.15: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? For her hair is given to her for a covering.
ESV.16: If anyone is inclined to be contentious, we have no such practice, nor do the churches of God.
ESV.17: But in the following instructions I do not commend you, because when you come together it is not for the better but for the worse.
ESV.18: For, in the first place, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you. And I believe it in part,
ESV.19: for there must be factions among you in order that those who are genuine among you may be recognized.
ESV.20: When you come together, it is not the Lord's supper that you eat.
ESV.21: For in eating, each one goes ahead with his own meal. One goes hungry, another gets drunk.
ESV.22: What! Do you not have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I commend you in this? No, I will not.
ESV.23: For I received from the Lord what I also delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night when he was betrayed took bread,
ESV.24: and when he had given thanks, he broke it, and said, “This is my body, which is for  you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
ESV.25: In the same way also he took the cup, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
ESV.26: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord's death until he comes.
ESV.27: Whoever, therefore, eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty concerning the body and blood of the Lord.
ESV.28: Let a person examine himself, then, and so eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
ESV.29: For anyone who eats and drinks without discerning the body eats and drinks judgment on himself.
ESV.30: That is why many of you are weak and ill, and some have died.
ESV.31: But if we judged  ourselves truly, we would not be judged.
ESV.32: But when we are judged by the Lord, we are disciplined  so that we may not be condemned along with the world.
ESV.33: So then, my brothers,  when you come together to eat, wait for  one another —
ESV.34: if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home — so that when you come together it will not be for judgment. About the other things I will give directions when I come.

NASB.1: Be imitators of me, just as I also am of Christ.
NASB.2: Now I praise you because you remember me in everything and hold firmly to the traditions, just as I delivered them to you.
NASB.3: But I want you to understand that Christ is the head of every man, and the man is the head of a woman, and God is the head of Christ.
NASB.4: Every man who has [something] on his head while praying or prophesying disgraces his head.
NASB.5: But every woman who has her head uncovered while praying or prophesying disgraces her head, for she is one and the same as the woman whose head is shaved.
NASB.6: For if a woman does not cover her head, let her also have her hair cut off; but if it is disgraceful for a woman to have her hair cut off or her head shaved, let her cover her head.
NASB.7: For a man ought not to have his head covered, since he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is the glory of man.
NASB.8: For man does not originate from woman, but woman from man;
NASB.9: for indeed man was not created for the woman's sake, but woman for the man's sake.
NASB.10: Therefore the woman ought to have [a symbol of] authority on her head, because of the angels.
NASB.11: However, in the Lord, neither is woman independent of man, nor is man independent of woman.
NASB.12: For as the woman originates from the man, so also the man [has his birth] through the woman; and all things originate from God.
NASB.13: Judge for yourselves: is it proper for a woman to pray to God [with her head] uncovered?
NASB.14: Does not even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him,
NASB.15: but if a woman has long hair, it is a glory to her? For her hair is given to her for a covering.
NASB.16: But if one is inclined to be contentious, we have no other practice, nor have the churches of God.
NASB.17: But in giving this instruction, I do not praise you, because you come together not for the better but for the worse.
NASB.18: For, in the first place, when you come together as a church, I hear that divisions exist among you; and in part I believe it.
NASB.19: For there must also be factions among you, so that those who are approved may become evident among you.
NASB.20: Therefore when you meet together, it is not to eat the Lord's Supper,
NASB.21: for in your eating each one takes his own supper first; and one is hungry and another is drunk.
NASB.22: What! Do you not have houses in which to eat and drink? Or do you despise the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you? In this I will not praise you.
NASB.23: For I received from the Lord that which I also delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus in the night in which He was betrayed took bread;
NASB.24: and when He had given thanks, He broke it and said, 'This is My body, which is for you; do this in remembrance of Me.'
NASB.25: In the same way [He took] the cup also after supper, saying, 'This cup is the new covenant in My blood; do this, as often as you drink [it], in remembrance of Me.'
NASB.26: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord's death until He comes.
NASB.27: Therefore whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner, shall be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord.
NASB.28: But a man must examine himself, and in so doing he is to eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
NASB.29: For he who eats and drinks, eats and drinks judgment to himself if he does not judge the body rightly.
NASB.30: For this reason many among you are weak and sick, and a number sleep.
NASB.31: But if we judged ourselves rightly, we would not be judged.
NASB.32: But when we are judged, we are disciplined by the Lord so that we will not be condemned along with the world.
NASB.33: So then, my brethren, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
NASB.34: If anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, so that you will not come together for judgment. The remaining matters I will arrange when I come.

NLT.1: And you should imitate me, just as I imitate Christ.
NLT.2: I am so glad that you always keep me in your thoughts, and that you are following the teachings I passed on to you.
NLT.3: But there is one thing I want you to know: The head of every man is Christ, the head of woman is man, and the head of Christ is God.
NLT.4: A man dishonors his head if he covers his head while praying or prophesying.
NLT.5: But a woman dishonors her head if she prays or prophesies without a covering on her head, for this is the same as shaving her head.
NLT.6: Yes, if she refuses to wear a head covering, she should cut off all her hair! But since it is shameful for a woman to have her hair cut or her head shaved, she should wear a covering.
NLT.7: A man should not wear anything on his head when worshiping, for man is made in God’s image and reflects God’s glory. And woman reflects man’s glory.
NLT.8: For the first man didn’t come from woman, but the first woman came from man.
NLT.9: And man was not made for woman, but woman was made for man.
NLT.10: For this reason, and because the angels are watching, a woman should wear a covering on her head to show she is under authority.
NLT.11: But among the Lord’s people, women are not independent of men, and men are not independent of women.
NLT.12: For although the first woman came from man, every other man was born from a woman, and everything comes from God.
NLT.13: Judge for yourselves. Is it right for a woman to pray to God in public without covering her head?
NLT.14: Isn’t it obvious that it’s disgraceful for a man to have long hair?
NLT.15: And isn’t long hair a woman’s pride and joy? For it has been given to her as a covering.
NLT.16: But if anyone wants to argue about this, I simply say that we have no other custom than this, and neither do God’s other churches.
NLT.17: But in the following instructions, I cannot praise you. For it sounds as if more harm than good is done when you meet together.
NLT.18: First, I hear that there are divisions among you when you meet as a church, and to some extent I believe it.
NLT.19: But, of course, there must be divisions among you so that you who have God’s approval will be recognized!
NLT.20: When you meet together, you are not really interested in the Lord’s Supper.
NLT.21: For some of you hurry to eat your own meal without sharing with others. As a result, some go hungry while others get drunk.
NLT.22: What? Don’t you have your own homes for eating and drinking? Or do you really want to disgrace God’s church and shame the poor? What am I supposed to say? Do you want me to praise you? Well, I certainly will not praise you for this!
NLT.23: For I pass on to you what I received from the Lord himself. On the night when he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took some bread
NLT.24: and gave thanks to God for it. Then he broke it in pieces and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
NLT.25: In the same way, he took the cup of wine after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant between God and his people — an agreement confirmed with my blood. Do this in remembrance of me as often as you drink it.”
NLT.26: For every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you are announcing the Lord’s death until he comes again.
NLT.27: So anyone who eats this bread or drinks this cup of the Lord unworthily is guilty of sinning against the body and blood of the Lord.
NLT.28: That is why you should examine yourself before eating the bread and drinking the cup.
NLT.29: For if you eat the bread or drink the cup without honoring the body of Christ, you are eating and drinking God’s judgment upon yourself.
NLT.30: That is why many of you are weak and sick and some have even died.
NLT.31: But if we would examine ourselves, we would not be judged by God in this way.
NLT.32: Yet when we are judged by the Lord, we are being disciplined so that we will not be condemned along with the world.
NLT.33: So, my dear brothers and sisters, when you gather for the Lord’s Supper, wait for each other.
NLT.34: If you are really hungry, eat at home so you won’t bring judgment upon yourselves when you meet together. I’ll give you instructions about the other matters after I arrive.

WEB.1: Be imitators of me, even as I also am of Christ.
WEB.2: Now I praise you, brothers, that you remember me in all things, and hold firm the traditions, even as I delivered them to you.
WEB.3: But I would have you know that the head  of every man is Christ, and the head  of the woman is man, and the head  of Christ is God.
WEB.4: Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonors his head.
WEB.5: But every woman praying or prophesying with her head uncovered dishonors her head. For it is one and the same thing as if she were shaved.
WEB.6: For if a woman is not covered, let her hair also be cut off. But if it is shameful for a woman to have her hair cut off or be shaved, let her be covered.
WEB.7: For a man indeed ought not to have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God, but the woman is the glory of the man.
WEB.8: For man is not from woman, but woman from man;
WEB.9: for man wasn’t created for the woman, but woman for the man.
WEB.10: For this cause the woman ought to have authority over her own head, because of the angels.
WEB.11: Nevertheless, neither is the woman independent of the man, nor the man independent of the woman, in the Lord.
WEB.12: For as woman came from man, so a man also comes through a woman; but all things are from God.
WEB.13: Judge for yourselves. Is it appropriate that a woman pray to God unveiled?
WEB.14: Doesn’t even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him?
WEB.15: But if a woman has long hair, it is a glory to her, for her hair is given to her for a covering.
WEB.16: But if any man seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, neither do God’s assemblies.
WEB.17: But in giving you this command, I don’t praise you, that you come together not for the better but for the worse.
WEB.18: For first of all, when you come together in the assembly, I hear that divisions exist among you, and I partly believe it.
WEB.19: For there also must be factions among you, that those who are approved may be revealed among you.
WEB.20: When therefore you assemble yourselves together, it is not the Lord’s supper that you eat.
WEB.21: For in your eating each one takes his own supper first. One is hungry, and another is drunken.
WEB.22: What, don’t you have houses to eat and to drink in? Or do you despise God’s assembly, and put them to shame who don’t have? What shall I tell you? Shall I praise you? In this I don’t praise you.
WEB.23: For I received from the Lord that which also I delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night in which he was betrayed took bread.
WEB.24: When he had given thanks, he broke it, and said, “Take, eat. This is my body, which is broken for you. Do this in memory of me.”
WEB.25: In the same way he also took the cup, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink, in memory of me.”
WEB.26: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.
WEB.27: Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks the Lord’s cup in a way unworthy of the Lord will be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord.
WEB.28: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of the bread, and drink of the cup.
WEB.29: For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy way eats and drinks judgment to himself, if he doesn’t discern the Lord’s body.
WEB.30: For this cause many among you are weak and sickly, and not a few sleep.
WEB.31: For if we discerned ourselves, we wouldn’t be judged.
WEB.32: But when we are judged, we are punished by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.
WEB.33: Therefore, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
WEB.34: But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest your coming together be for judgment. The rest I will set in order whenever I come.

CSB17.1: Imitate me, as I also imitate Christ. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.2: Now I praise you <sup>[1]</sup> because you remember me in everything and hold fast to the traditions <sup>ⓜ</sup> just as I delivered <sup>ⓝ</sup> them to you.
CSB17.3: But I want you to know that Christ is the head <sup>ⓐ</sup> of every man, and the man is the head of the woman, <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> and God is the head of Christ. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.4: Every man who prays or prophesies with something on his head dishonors <sup>ⓓ</sup> his head.
CSB17.5: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered dishonors her head, since that is one and the same as having her head shaved. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.6: For if a woman doesn’t cover her head, she should have her hair cut off. But if it is disgraceful for a woman to have her hair cut off or her head shaved, let her head be covered.
CSB17.7: A man should not cover his head, because he is the image <sup>ⓕ</sup> and glory of God. <sup>ⓖ</sup> So too, woman is the glory of man.
CSB17.8: For man did not come from woman, but woman came from man. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.9: Neither was man created for the sake of woman, but woman for the sake of man.
CSB17.10: This is why a woman should have a symbol of authority on her head, because of the angels.
CSB17.11: In the Lord, however, woman is not independent of man, and man is not independent of woman.
CSB17.12: For just as woman came from man, so man comes through woman, and all things come from God. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.13: Judge <sup>ⓙ</sup> for yourselves: Is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?
CSB17.14: Does not even nature itself teach <sup>ⓚ</sup> you that if a man has long hair it is a disgrace <sup>ⓛ</sup> to him,
CSB17.15: but that if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? <sup>ⓜ</sup> For her hair is given to her <sup>[3]</sup> as a covering.
CSB17.16: If anyone wants to argue about this, we have no other <sup>[4]</sup> custom, nor do the churches of God.
CSB17.17: Now in giving this instruction I do not praise you, since you come together not for the better but for the worse.
CSB17.18: For to begin with, I hear that when you come together as a church there are divisions among you, and in part I believe it.
CSB17.19: Indeed, it is necessary that there be factions <sup>ⓝ</sup> among you, so that those who are approved may be recognized among you.
CSB17.20: When you come together, then, it is not to eat the Lord’s Supper. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.21: For at the meal, each one eats his own supper. <sup>[5]</sup> So one person is hungry while another gets drunk!
CSB17.22: Don’t you have homes in which to eat and drink? Or do you despise the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What should I say to you? Should I praise you? I do not praise you in this matter!
CSB17.23: For I received from the Lord what I also passed on to you: <sup>ⓑ</sup> On the night when he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread,
CSB17.24: and when he had given thanks, broke it, and said, <sup>[6]</sup> “This is my body, which is <sup>[7]</sup>for you. Do this in remembrance of me.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.25: In the same way also he took the cup, after supper, and said, “This cup is the new covenant <sup>ⓓ</sup> in my blood. <sup>ⓔ</sup> Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
CSB17.26: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death <sup>ⓕ</sup> until he comes. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.27: So then, whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of sin against the body <sup>[8]</sup> and blood of the Lord.
CSB17.28: Let a person examine himself; in this way let him eat the bread and drink from the cup.
CSB17.29: For whoever eats and drinks without recognizing the body, <sup>[9]</sup> eats and drinks judgment <sup>ⓗ</sup> on himself.
CSB17.30: This is why many are sick and ill among you, and many have fallen asleep. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.31: If we were properly judging ourselves, we would not be judged,
CSB17.32: but when we are judged <sup>ⓙ</sup> by the Lord, we are disciplined, so that we may not be condemned <sup>ⓚ</sup> with the world.
CSB17.33: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, when you come together to eat, welcome one another. <sup>[10]</sup>
CSB17.34: If anyone is hungry, he should eat at home, so that when you gather together you will not come under judgment. I will give instructions about the other matters whenever I come. <sup>ⓛ</sup>

AMP.1: Imitate me, just as I imitate Christ.
AMP.2: I praise and appreciate you because you remember me in everything and you firmly hold to the traditions [the substance of my instructions], just as I have passed them on to you.
AMP.3: But I want you to understand that Christ is the head (authority over) of every man, and man is the head of woman, and God is the head of Christ.
AMP.4: Every man who prays or prophesies with something on his head dishonors his head [and the One who is his head].
AMP.5: And every woman who prays or prophesies when she has her head uncovered disgraces her head; for she is one and the same as the woman whose head is shaved [in disgrace].
AMP.6: If a woman does not cover her head, she should have her hair cut off; and if it is disgraceful for a woman to have her hair cut off or her head shaved, she should cover her head.
AMP.7: A man ought not have his head covered [during worship], since he is the image and [reflected] glory of God; but the woman is [the expression of] man's glory.
AMP.8: For man does not originate from woman, but woman from man;
AMP.9: for indeed man was not created for the sake of woman, but woman for the sake of man.
AMP.10: Therefore the woman ought to have a sign of authority on her head, for the sake of the angels [so as not to offend them].
AMP.11: Nevertheless, woman is not independent of man, nor is man independent of woman.
AMP.12: For as the woman originates from the man, so also man is born through the woman; and all things [whether male or female] originate from God [as their Creator].
AMP.13: Judge for yourselves; is it proper for a woman to offer prayer to God [publicly] with her head uncovered?
AMP.14: Does not common sense itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him,
AMP.15: but if a woman has long hair, it is her ornament and glory? For her long hair is given to her as a covering.
AMP.16: Now if anyone is inclined to be contentious [about this], we have no other practice [in worship than this], nor do the churches of God [in general].
AMP.17: But in giving this next instruction, I do not praise you, because when you meet together it is not for the better but for the worse.
AMP.18: For, in the first place, when you meet together in church, I hear that there are divisions among you; and in part I believe it,
AMP.19: for [doubtless] there have to be factions among you, so that those who are of approved character may be clearly recognized among you.
AMP.20: So when you meet together, it is not to eat the Lord's Supper,
AMP.21: for when you eat, each one hurries to get his own supper first [not waiting for others or the poor]. So one goes hungry while another gets drunk.
AMP.22: What! Do you not have houses in which to eat and drink? Or do you show contempt for the church of God and humiliate those [impoverished believers] who have nothing? What will I say to you? Shall I praise you for this? In this I will not praise you!
AMP.23: For I received from the Lord Himself that [instruction] which I passed on to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night in which He was betrayed took bread;
AMP.24: and when He had given thanks, He broke it and said, "This is (represents) My body, which is [offered as a sacrifice] for you. Do this in [affectionate] remembrance of Me."
AMP.25: In the same way, after supper He took the cup, saying, "This cup is the new covenant [ratified and established] in My blood; do this, as often as you drink it, in [affectionate] remembrance of Me."
AMP.26: For every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you are [symbolically] proclaiming [the fact of] the Lord's death until He comes [again].
AMP.27: So then whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in a way that is unworthy [of Him] will be guilty of [profaning and sinning against] the body and blood of the Lord.
AMP.28: But a person must [prayerfully] examine himself [and his relationship to Christ], and only when he has done so should he eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
AMP.29: For anyone who eats and drinks [without solemn reverence and heartfelt gratitude for the sacrifice of Christ], eats and drinks a judgment on himself if he does not recognize the body [of Christ].
AMP.30: That [careless and unworthy participation] is the reason why many among you are weak and sick, and a number sleep [in death].
AMP.31: But if we evaluated and judged ourselves honestly [recognizing our shortcomings and correcting our behavior], we would not be judged.
AMP.32: But when we [fall short and] are judged by the Lord, we are disciplined [by undergoing His correction] so that we will not be condemned [to eternal punishment] along with the world.
AMP.33: So then, my brothers and sisters, when you come together to eat [the Lord's Supper], wait for one another [and see to it that no one is left out].
AMP.34: If anyone is too hungry [to wait], let him eat at home, so that you will not come together for judgment [on yourselves]. About the remaining matters [of which I was informed], I will take care of them when I come.

MSG.1: It pleases me that you continue to remember and honor me by keeping up the traditions of the faith I taught you. All actual authority stems from Christ.
MSG.3: In a marriage relationship, there is authority from Christ to husband, and from husband to wife. The authority of Christ is the authority of God.
MSG.4: Any man who speaks with God or about God in a way that shows a lack of respect for the authority of Christ, dishonors Christ.
MSG.5: In the same way, a wife who speaks with God in a way that shows a lack of respect for the authority of her husband, dishonors her husband.
MSG.6: Worse, she dishonors herself—an ugly sight, like a woman with her head shaved. This is basically the origin of these customs we have of women wearing head coverings in worship, while men take their hats off. By these symbolic acts,
MSG.7: men and women, who far too often butt heads with each other, submit their "heads" to the Head: God.
MSG.10: Don't, by the way, read too much into the differences here between men and women.
MSG.11: Neither man nor woman can go it alone or claim priority. Man was created first, as a beautiful shining reflection of God—that is true. But the head on a woman's body clearly outshines in beauty the head of her "head," her husband.
MSG.12: The first woman came from man, true—but ever since then, every man comes from a woman! And since virtually everything comes from God anyway, let's quit going through these "who's first" routines.
MSG.13: Don't you agree there is something naturally powerful in the symbolism—a woman, her beautiful hair reminiscent of angels, praying in adoration; a man, his head bared in reverence, praying in submission?
MSG.16: I hope you're not going to be argumentative about this. All God's churches see it this way; I don't want you standing out as an exception.
MSG.17: Regarding this next item, I'm not at all pleased. I am getting the picture that when you meet together it brings out your worst side instead of your best!
MSG.18: First, I get this report on your divisiveness, competing with and criticizing each other. I'm reluctant to believe it, but there it is.
MSG.19: The best that can be said for it is that the testing process will bring truth into the open and confirm it.
MSG.20: And then I find that you bring your divisions to worship—you come together, and instead of eating the Lord's Supper,
MSG.21: you bring in a lot of food from the outside and make pigs of yourselves. Some are left out, and go home hungry. Others have to be carried out, too drunk to walk. I can't believe it!
MSG.22: Don't you have your own homes to eat and drink in? Why would you stoop to desecrating God's church? Why would you actually shame God's poor? I never would have believed you would stoop to this. And I'm not going to stand by and say nothing.
MSG.23: Let me go over with you again exactly what goes on in the Lord's Supper and why it is so centrally important. I received my instructions from the Master himself and passed them on to you. The Master, Jesus, on the night of his betrayal, took bread.
MSG.24: Having given thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, broken for you. Do this to remember me.
MSG.25: After supper, he did the same thing with the cup: This cup is my blood, my new covenant with you. Each time you drink this cup, remember me.
MSG.26: What you must solemnly realize is that every time you eat this bread and every time you drink this cup, you reenact in your words and actions the death of the Master. You will be drawn back to this meal again and again until the Master returns. You must never let familiarity breed contempt.
MSG.27: Anyone who eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Master irreverently is like part of the crowd that jeered and spit on him at his death. Is that the kind of "remembrance" you want to be part of?
MSG.28: Examine your motives, test your heart, come to this meal in holy awe.
MSG.29: If you give no thought (or worse, don't care) about the broken body of the Master when you eat and drink, you're running the risk of serious consequences.
MSG.30: That's why so many of you even now are listless and sick, and others have gone to an early grave.
MSG.31: If we get this straight now, we won't have to be straightened out later on.
MSG.32: Better to be confronted by the Master now than to face a fiery confrontation later.
MSG.33: So, my friends, when you come together to the Lord's Table, be reverent and courteous with one another.
MSG.34: If you're so hungry that you can't wait to be served, go home and get a sandwich. But by no means risk turning this Meal into an eating and drinking binge or a family squabble. It is a spiritual meal—a love feast. The other things you asked about, I'll respond to in person when I make my next visit.

NIV.1: 11 Follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ.
NIV.2:
Propriety in Worship
I praise you for remembering me in everything and for holding to the teachings, just as I passed them on to you.
NIV.3: Now I want you to realize that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is man, and the head of Christ is God.
NIV.4: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered dishonors his head.
NIV.5: And every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered dishonors her head—it is just as though her head were shaved.
NIV.6: If a woman does not cover her head, she should have her hair cut off; and if it is a disgrace for a woman to have her hair cut or shaved off, she should cover her head.
NIV.7: A man ought not to cover his head, since he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is the glory of man.
NIV.8: For man did not come from woman, but woman from man;
NIV.9: neither was man created for woman, but woman for man.
NIV.10: For this reason, and because of the angels, the woman ought to have a sign of authority on her head.
NIV.11: In the Lord, however, woman is not independent of man, nor is man independent of woman.
NIV.12: For as woman came from man, so also man is born of woman. But everything comes from God.
NIV.13: Judge for yourselves: Is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?
NIV.14: Does not the very nature of things teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him,
NIV.15: but that if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? For long hair is given to her as a covering.
NIV.16: If anyone wants to be contentious about this, we have no other practice—nor do the churches of God.
NIV.17:
The Lord’s Supper
In the following directives I have no praise for you, for your meetings do more harm than good.
NIV.18: In the first place, I hear that when you come together as a church, there are divisions among you, and to some extent I believe it.
NIV.19: No doubt there have to be differences among you to show which of you have God’s approval.
NIV.20: When you come together, it is not the Lord’s Supper you eat,
NIV.21: for as you eat, each of you goes ahead without waiting for anybody else. One remains hungry, another gets drunk.
NIV.22: Don’t you have homes to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you for this? Certainly not!
NIV.23: For I received from the Lord what I also passed on to you: The Lord Jesus, on the night he was betrayed, took bread,
NIV.24: and when he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is for you; do this in remembrance of me.”
NIV.25: In the same way, after supper he took the cup, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood; do this, whenever you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
NIV.26: For whenever you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.
NIV.27: Therefore, whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of sinning against the body and blood of the Lord.
NIV.28: A man ought to examine himself before he eats of the bread and drinks of the cup.
NIV.29: For anyone who eats and drinks without recognizing the body of the Lord eats and drinks judgment on himself.
NIV.30: That is why many among you are weak and sick, and a number of you have fallen asleep.
NIV.31: But if we judged ourselves, we would not come under judgment.
NIV.32: When we are judged by the Lord, we are being disciplined so that we will not be condemned with the world.
NIV.33: So then, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for each other.
NIV.34:
If anyone is hungry, he should eat at home, so that when you meet together it may not result in judgment.
And when I come I will give further directions.

NRSVCE.1: Be imitators of me, as I am of Christ.
NRSVCE.2: I commend you because you remember me in everything and maintain the traditions just as I handed them on to you.
NRSVCE.3: But I want you to understand that Christ is the head of every man, and the husband is the head of his wife, and God is the head of Christ.
NRSVCE.4: Any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head disgraces his head,
NRSVCE.5: but any woman who prays or prophesies with her head unveiled disgraces her head — it is one and the same thing as having her head shaved.
NRSVCE.6: For if a woman will not veil herself, then she should cut off her hair; but if it is disgraceful for a woman to have her hair cut off or to be shaved, she should wear a veil.
NRSVCE.7: For a man ought not to have his head veiled, since he is the image and reflection of God; but woman is the reflection of man.
NRSVCE.8: Indeed, man was not made from woman, but woman from man.
NRSVCE.9: Neither was man created for the sake of woman, but woman for the sake of man.
NRSVCE.10: For this reason a woman ought to have a symbol of authority on her head, because of the angels.
NRSVCE.11: Nevertheless, in the Lord woman is not independent of man or man independent of woman.
NRSVCE.12: For just as woman came from man, so man comes through woman; but all things come from God.
NRSVCE.13: Judge for yourselves: is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head unveiled?
NRSVCE.14: Does not nature itself teach you that if a man wears long hair, it is degrading to him,
NRSVCE.15: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? For her hair is given to her for a covering.
NRSVCE.16: But if anyone is disposed to be contentious — we have no such custom, nor do the churches of God.
NRSVCE.17: Now in the following instructions I do not commend you, because when you come together it is not for the better but for the worse.
NRSVCE.18: For, to begin with, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you; and to some extent I believe it.
NRSVCE.19: Indeed, there have to be factions among you, for only so will it become clear who among you are genuine.
NRSVCE.20: When you come together, it is not really to eat the Lord’s supper.
NRSVCE.21: For when the time comes to eat, each of you goes ahead with your own supper, and one goes hungry and another becomes drunk.
NRSVCE.22: What! Do you not have homes to eat and drink in? Or do you show contempt for the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What should I say to you? Should I commend you? In this matter I do not commend you!
NRSVCE.23: For I received from the Lord what I also handed on to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night when he was betrayed took a loaf of bread,
NRSVCE.24: and when he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body that is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
NRSVCE.25: In the same way he took the cup also, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
NRSVCE.26: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.
NRSVCE.27: Whoever, therefore, eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be answerable for the body and blood of the Lord.
NRSVCE.28: Examine yourselves, and only then eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
NRSVCE.29: For all who eat and drink without discerning the body, eat and drink judgment against themselves.
NRSVCE.30: For this reason many of you are weak and ill, and some have died.
NRSVCE.31: But if we judged ourselves, we would not be judged.
NRSVCE.32: But when we are judged by the Lord, we are disciplined so that we may not be condemned along with the world.
NRSVCE.33: So then, my brothers and sisters, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
NRSVCE.34: If you are hungry, eat at home, so that when you come together, it will not be for your condemnation. About the other things I will give instructions when I come.

NET.1: Be imitators of me, just as I also am of Christ.
NET.2: I praise you because you remember me in everything and maintain the traditions just as I passed them on to you.
NET.3: But I want you to know that Christ is the head of every man, and the man is the head of a woman, and God is the head of Christ.
NET.4: Any man who prays or prophesies with his head covered disgraces his head.
NET.5: But any woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head, for it is one and the same thing as having a shaved head.
NET.6: For if a woman will not cover her head, she should cut off her hair. But if it is disgraceful for a woman to have her hair cut off or her head shaved, she should cover her head.
NET.7: For a man should not have his head covered, since he is the image and glory of God. But the woman is the glory of the man.
NET.8: For man did not come from woman, but woman from man.
NET.9: Neither was man created for the sake of woman, but woman for man.
NET.10: For this reason a woman should have a symbol of authority on her head, because of the angels.
NET.11: In any case, in the Lord woman is not independent of man, nor is man independent of woman.
NET.12: For just as woman came from man, so man comes through woman. But all things come from God.
NET.13: Judge for yourselves: Is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?
NET.14: Does not nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace for him,
NET.15: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? For her hair is given to her for a covering.
NET.16: If anyone intends to quarrel about this, we have no other practice, nor do the churches of God.
NET.17: Now in giving the following instruction I do not praise you, because you come together not for the better but for the worse.
NET.18: For in the first place, when you come together as a church I hear there are divisions among you, and in part I believe it.
NET.19: For there must in fact be divisions among you, so that those of you who are approved may be evident.
NET.20: Now when you come together at the same place, you are not really eating the Lord’s Supper.
NET.21: For when it is time to eat, everyone proceeds with his own supper. One is hungry and another becomes drunk.
NET.22: Do you not have houses so that you can eat and drink? Or are you trying to show contempt for the church of God by shaming those who have nothing? What should I say to you? Should I praise you? I will not praise you for this!
NET.23: For I received from the Lord what I also passed on to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night in which he was betrayed took bread,
NET.24: and after he had given thanks he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
NET.25: In the same way, he also took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, every time you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
NET.26: For every time you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.
NET.27: For this reason, whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
NET.28: A person should examine himself first, and in this way let him eat the bread and drink of the cup.
NET.29: For the one who eats and drinks without careful regard for the body eats and drinks judgment against himself.
NET.30: That is why many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few are dead.
NET.31: But if we examined ourselves, we would not be judged.
NET.32: But when we are judged by the Lord, we are disciplined so that we may not be condemned with the world.
NET.33: So then, my brothers and sisters, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
NET.34: If anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, so that when you assemble it does not lead to judgment. I will give directions about other matters when I come.
